q:
Fill in the missing relative pronoun ('who' or 'that') to correct the sentence:
The woman lives next door is a doctor.
a: The woman who lives next door is a doctor.

q:
Fill in the missing relative pronoun ('who' or 'that') to correct the sentence:
The people work in the office are very friendly.
a: The people who work in the office are very friendly.

q:
Fill in the missing relative pronoun ('who' or 'that') to correct the sentence:
What happened to the money was on the table?
a: What happened to the money that was on the table?

q:
Correct the relative pronoun structure in the following sentence:
What's the best thing it has ever happened to you?
a: What's the best thing that has ever happened to you?

q:
Complete the sentence with an appropriate relative clause based on the context. Context: A friend is wearing a dress. You like it. You tell her:
I like the dress ___.
a: I like the dress you are wearing.

q:
Complete the sentence with an appropriate relative clause based on the context. Context: A friend is going to the cinema. You want to know the name of the film. You say:
What's the name of the film ___?
a: What's the name of the film you are going to see?

q:
Complete the sentence with an appropriate relative clause based on the context. Context: You wanted to visit a museum, but it was shut. You tell a friend:
The museum ___ was shut.
a: The museum we wanted to visit was shut.

q:
Complete the sentence with an appropriate relative clause based on the context. Context: You invited people to your party. Some of them couldn't come. You tell someone:
Some of the people ___ couldn't come.
a: Some of the people I invited to my party couldn't come.

q:
Complete the sentence with an appropriate relative clause based on the context. Context: Your friend had to do some work. You want to know if she has finished. You say:
Have you finished the work ___?
a: Have you finished the work you had to do?

q:
Complete the sentence with an appropriate relative clause based on the context. Context: You rented a car. It broke down after a few miles. You tell a friend:
Unfortunately the car ___ broke down after a few miles.
a: Unfortunately the car I rented broke down after a few miles.

q:
Order the words in parentheses to form a relative clause with a preposition:
We couldn't go to (we / invited / to / were / the wedding).
a: We couldn't go to the wedding we were invited to.

q:
Order the words in parentheses to form a relative clause with a preposition:
What's the name of (the hotel / about / me / told / you)?
a: What's the name of the hotel you told me about?

q:
Order the words in parentheses to form a relative clause with a preposition:
Unfortunately I didn't get (applied / I / the job / for).
a: Unfortunately I didn't get the job I applied for.

q:
Order the words in parentheses to form a relative clause with a preposition:
Did you enjoy (you / the concert / to / went)?
a: Did you enjoy the concert you went to?

q:
Order the words in parentheses to form a relative clause with a preposition:
Gary is a good person to know. He's (on / rely / can / somebody / you).
a: Gary is a good person to know. He's somebody you can rely on.

q:
Order the words in parentheses to form a relative clause with a preposition:
Who were (the people / with / were / you) in the restaurant yesterday?
a: Who were the people you were with in the restaurant yesterday?

q:
Fill in the blank with 'that' or 'what':
Did you hear ___ they said?
a: Did you hear what they said?

q:
Fill in the blank with 'that' or 'what':
She gives her children everything ___ they want.
a: She gives her children everything that they want.

q:
Fill in the blank with 'that' or 'what':
Tell me ___ you want and I'll try to get it for you.
a: Tell me what you want and I'll try to get it for you.

q:
Fill in the blank with 'that' or 'what':
Why do you blame me for everything ___ goes wrong?
a: Why do you blame me for everything that goes wrong?

q:
Fill in the blank with 'that' or 'what':
I won't be able to do much, but I'll do ___ I can.
a: I won't be able to do much, but I'll do what I can.

q:
Fill in the blank with 'that' or 'what':
I won't be able to do much, but I'll do the best ___ I can.
a: I won't be able to do much, but I'll do the best that I can.

q:
Fill in the blank with 'that' or 'what':
I don't agree with ___ you said.
a: I don't agree with what you said.

q:
Fill in the blank with 'that' or 'what':
I don't trust him. I don't believe anything ___ he says.
a: I don't trust him. I don't believe anything that he says.
